article_title: EU Council and Parliament Advance AI Act Overhaul, Delaying High-Risk Rules as Civil Society Warns of Rights Rollback

content_preview.title: EU Council and Parliament Advance AI Act Overhaul, Delaying High-Risk Rules as Civil Society Warns of Rights Rollback
content_preview.summary: The EU Council and Parliament endorsed amendments delaying high-risk AI system deadlines by up to 16 months and banning nonconsensual deepfake imagery, as Amnesty International warns of a rights rollback.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Union is moving to reshape its landmark AI Act less than two years after its adoption, with both the Council of the EU and the European Parliament endorsing amendments that delay compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems and introduce new prohibitions on nonconsensual sexually explicit deepfakes. The changes form part of the bloc's broader "Omnibus VII" simplification package, which the European Commission framed as a bid to reduce regulatory burden and boost compet...

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough and balanced policy article covering both legislative progress and civil society pushback. Excellent structure with clear timeline.
editor_notes.source_verification: European Parliament: verified — vote 101-9-8, Kokalari quote, McNamara on nudification, watermarking deadline all confirmed. Amnesty International: verified — rights rollback critique, grandfathering concerns, call to strengthen regulations confirmed. Burges Salmon: verified — Council March 13 position, all deadline dates, CSAM ban, SME/mid-cap extension, sandbox deadline, trilogue timeline all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All dates, vote counts, quotes, and provisions verified against sources. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Presents both pro-business and civil rights perspectives without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior EU AI Act Omnibus VII coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality policy reporting with excellent source diversity (official EU, civil society, legal analysis). Approved.
